summary: Completed testing of Multi-language Translation Feature. All 13 backend tests passed. Frontend UI verified - Project edit dialog shows language configuration, Blog view dialog shows TranslationPanel, Public page shows LanguageSwitcher. Fixed bug in blog.py where translations field was not included in response.

issues: Minor: Missing aria-describedby for DialogContent (accessibility warning)

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/languages returns all 27 supported languages
Backend: GET /api/projects/{pid} returns languages and primary_language fields
Backend: PUT /api/projects/{pid} accepts languages and primary_language fields
Backend: GET /api/public/projects/{pid} returns languages and primary_language
Backend: Blog entry response includes 'translations' dict field
Backend: POST translate returns 400 if target_language not in project languages
Backend: POST translate returns 400 if no OpenAI key configured
Backend: Public blog list accepts ?lang= param and returns translated content
Backend: Library entry response includes 'translations' dict field
Backend: Public library list accepts ?lang= param
Backend: Diary entry response includes 'translations' dict field
Frontend: Project edit dialog shows language configuration with checkboxes
Frontend: Project edit dialog shows primary language dropdown
Frontend: BlogPage view dialog shows TranslationPanel with translate buttons
Frontend: Public page shows LanguageSwitcher with language options (en, nl, de)
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: /app/backend/routes/blog.py - build_blog_response() was missing translations field in response. Added translations=entry.get('translations', {})
translation.py: SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES dict correctly contains 27 languages
TranslationPanel.jsx: Well-structured component with proper data-testid attributes for testing
LanguageSwitcher.jsx: Correctly uses sessionStorage to persist language preference per project
public.py: Correctly applies translations when ?lang= param is provided
seed_data_creation: None - used existing admin account and Community Garden project
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-language translation feature fully implemented. GET /api/languages returns 27 supported languages. Projects have languages[] and primary_language fields. Translation endpoints: POST /api/projects/{pid}/blog/{eid}/translate (create via OpenAI), PUT .../translate/{lang} (manual edit), DELETE .../translate/{lang}. Same pattern for library and diary. Public endpoints accept ?lang= param to return translated content. Frontend: TranslationPanel component shows in Blog/Library/Diary view dialogs when project has multiple languages. LanguageSwitcher on public page stores preference in sessionStorage. Note: Actual OpenAI translation requires user to configure API key in Settings - without key, translate endpoint returns 400.
